mechanism_narrative: With FEDFUNDS pinned near zero in the aftermath of the 2008 crisis, continued near-zero or declining policy rates compress the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically supporting valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level/trend and 20d forward returns stays below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or returns during ZIRP periods are not significantly higher than the full-sample average (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid month-over-month acceleration in UNRATE signals a sharp deterioration in labor market conditions that triggers immediate risk-off repricing by equity investors, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 7.2% and rising rapidly through late 2008
failure_condition: SPX produces non-negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where the UNRATE trigger fires, over a rolling 2-year evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts (HOUST) show a month-over-month increase while the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains near zero, the joint signal indicates a housing-sector trough being supported by extraordinarily accommodative policy, foreshadowing a broader demand-side recovery that lifts equities over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises month-over-month while FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 625k, near historic lows in Dec 2008
failure_condition: In periods where both conditions jointly hold, 60-day forward returns are negative or statistically indistinguishable from zero (t-test p > 0.05) across a 3-year rolling evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: Continued month-over-month contraction in INDPRO during an NBER-dated recession regime signals ongoing supply-side collapse and corporate earnings deterioration, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns; the same INDPRO decline outside a recession regime does not carry the same negative implication.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 106.1173, declining sharply into late 2008
failure_condition: During recession-regime periods, 20-day forward returns conditional on INDPRO decline are not significantly more negative than during expansion-regime periods with equivalent INDPRO declines (difference-in-means p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month uptick in RSAFS (retail sales) signals resilient consumer demand that supports corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month regardless of the broader macro regime.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 343,242 million as of Dec 2008
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS month-over-month change and 20-day forward returns remains below 0.03 across any 2-year rolling walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: When monthly nonfarm payroll losses (PAYEMS) exceed 500,000, the scale of labor destruction signals a deepening recession that depresses consumer spending and corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 135,489 thousand with large monthly declines through late 2008
failure_condition: SPX produces positive or flat 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of instances where the payroll-loss trigger fires, evaluated over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is held near zero even as UNRATE continues to rise, the divergence signals maximal policy accommodation relative to labor deterioration, functioning as an implicit policy put that supports risk assets and produces positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS below 0.5% while UNRATE rises month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.16% and UNRATE at 7.2% and rising in Dec 2008/Jan 2009
regime_hint: monetary easing during labor market collapse
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns during periods satisfying the joint trigger are negative or statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.05) across a rolling 2-year evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: During a real GDP (GDPC1) contraction regime, negative or decelerating GDP growth signals broad economic weakness that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter; once GDP growth turns positive, the same growth-rate signal produces positive forward returns, making the relationship regime-dependent.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11,712.4 billion chained dollars, contracting through 2008
failure_condition: The sign of the correlation between GDPC1 growth and 60-day forward returns does not flip between contraction and expansion regimes when tested across multiple historical recession/expansion cycles, or the contraction-regime correlation is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).
